svg.title: Prolly Trees cover with a 3D version axis
svg.desc: A 5:2 blog cover showing Prolly trees as content-addressed ordered indexes. Three version slices sit on a diagonal time axis; each slice has its own root CID and small B+tree-like hierarchy, while green bridges show structurally shared subtrees across versions.

g.text: Leaves store data; internal slots route to child CIDs.
Version roots are tiny handles over a shared node space.
Use cases: snapshots, diff, merge, sync, audit history
Cheap history comes from root CIDs plus structural sharing.
Version axis: roots move through time, subtrees persist by CID
Each slice has its own root CID; green bridges are identical child CIDs reused across versions.

svg.text: green bridges = same CID across version slices

g.text: changed subtree gets a new CID
root CID is the version handle
A Prolly tree versions ordered data by moving only the root handle while unchanged subtrees remain shared by content ID.
